image.alt: Engraving of a warrior king standing with a long spear and a large oval shield in traditional dress

rounds.revealFact: Shaka transformed Zulu warfare with the short stabbing spear and new tactics, forging a small clan into a powerful kingdom in southern Africa.

image.alt: Painting of mounted commanders in tricorne hats and armor on a battlefield with cavalry clashing behind them

clues: An allied army made a bold, secret march of hundreds of miles to surprise a great power's forces beside a river.
The stunning victory shattered that power's aura of invincibility in a long war over a disputed throne.

pairs.a: The zipper is patented in its modern form
The first mechanical typewriter goes on sale

pairs.b: Coca-Cola is first sold as a fountain drink

pairs.a: The first home refrigerator goes on sale
Sliced bread is first sold in stores

eventQuestion.prompt: What is shown in this photograph?
options: The building of the transcontinental railroad

followUpQuestion.prompt: The goldfields these prospectors sought lay in which territory?

rounds.prompt: The Brooklyn Bridge opens, then the longest suspension bridge in the world.
rounds.revealFact: When it opened in 1883, the Brooklyn Bridge was the longest suspension bridge in the world and a wonder of engineering.

rounds.prompt: Where did a mound-building people raise a great earthwork city crowned by an enormous flat-topped pyramid of packed earth?
rounds.revealFact: Cahokia was the largest pre-Columbian city north of Mexico, home to tens of thousands and dominated by Monks Mound, a massive earthen pyramid.
